title: cerastium arvense subsp. arvum
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. arvum, also known as Field chickweed, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and hairy, oval-shaped leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Field chickweed include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin issues and gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of Field chickweed can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
